features.title: Cross-session memory and feature list infrastructure
features.acceptance: claude-progress.txt, feature-list.json, init.sh, session-start.sh, session-end.sh exist and tests pass

features.title: Strongly-worded constraints in CLAUDE.md and agent definitions
features.acceptance: CLAUDE.md uses 'It is unacceptable to...' format, agent definitions include session/test/commit rules

features.title: Hook and script adjustments for test enforcement and git commits
features.acceptance: codex-implement.sh enforces testing, guard-bash-policy.sh allows new scripts

features.title: Migrate .claude/last-* to .claude/sessions/<id>/ scoped storage
features.acceptance: All gate/review artifacts written to .claude/sessions/<id>/. Legacy .claude/last-* paths eliminated from write paths. session-util.sh is sole accessor. All existing tests pass.

features.title: Connect boundary-test-map.json to deterministic resolver
features.acceptance: Given a changed file list, resolver returns exact test commands. No ambiguity in which boundary tests to run. codex-implement.sh uses resolver output.

features.title: Split orch-health into check (fast CI) and doctor (diagnostic)
features.acceptance: pnpm run check is fast (<2s) and CI-safe. pnpm run doctor gives detailed diagnostics. init.sh uses check.

features.title: Remove .claude/settings.local.json from tracked source of truth
features.acceptance: settings.local.json is in .gitignore. A canonical template or generation script exists. No test depends on tracked settings.local.json content.

features.title: Explicit packageManager, platform-clean install, bootstrap consolidation
features.acceptance: package.json has packageManager field. Fresh clone + pnpm install + pnpm test passes on clean env. init.sh verifies prerequisites.

features.title: P1 promotion: close gaps identified in external review
features.acceptance: 1) pnpm test runs hook shell tests. 2) session-end.sh does not downgrade done features. 3) test-session-scripts.sh handles missing git identity. 4) boundary resolver blocks in eval gate (not advisory). 5) model-router defaults are correct (review/retry=gpt-5.4-mini). 6) settings migration strips broad Bash wildcards.

features.title: Add evals/capability/ and evals/regression/ evaluation framework
features.acceptance: eval runner executes capability and regression evals. Results are machine-readable JSON.

features.title: Module ablation runner for harness component necessity testing
features.acceptance: Runner can disable individual harness modules and measure impact on eval scores.

features.title: Advanced model routing with capability-based selection
features.acceptance: Router selects model based on task complexity, file count, and historical performance.

features.title: Stable/candidate harness branch separation with promotion gates
features.acceptance: Harness changes go to candidate branch first. Promotion requires harness-evals PASS.

features.title: Evidence plane: verify-run.sh, manifest.json, non-forgeable execution evidence
features.acceptance: verify-run.sh wraps test execution and records exit code/log/hash. manifest.json is source of truth for eval runs. eval-runner.sh uses manifest for artifact persistence. boundary-results.json records boundary test evidence from wrapper, not model.

features.title: Bind evidence to current run and remove model-report fallback
features.acceptance: Current run_id recorded and available to gates. codex-eval-gate.sh reads only current-run manifest for boundary evidence. Missing/stale wrapper evidence fails closed (no model-report fallback). boundary-results.json produced by wrapper. Tests cover stale rejection and no-fallback.

features.title: Compile raw review text into structured blocker issues
features.acceptance: Review text converted to stable blocker IDs with severity/deferrable/acceptance/evidence. Worker consumes open-issues.json not raw prose.

features.title: Separate defer/phase/risk authority from worker execution
features.acceptance: Worker cannot directly set deferred/accepted-risk on blockers. Defer requests are structured JSON resolved by separate adjudication path.

features.title: Per-blocker resolution ledger
features.acceptance: Every blocker ID has ledger entry. Fixed entries reference evidence artifacts. Deferred entries require adjudicator approval. Promotion fails if any unresolved.

features.title: Reject prose-only retries after blocking review
features.acceptance: After blocking review, next attempt must include code diff, new evidence, or approved defer. Summary-only retries fail.

features.title: Split promotion/state mutation from session recording
features.acceptance: session-end no longer owns all progress/feature/VCS duties. Promotion is evidence-driven. Recording possible even when promotion fails.
